text: Both the Sales Comparison Approach and the Income Approach were considered applicable and were developed for the subject property. The Sales Comparison Approach was given primary weight as it most directly reflects market participant behavior and recent transactions of similar neighborhood retail assets. The Income Approach provided additional support to the value conclusion, reflecting market expectations for stabilized income-producing properties. The Cost Approach was not developed due to limited relevance for the subject’s property type and age. The final reconciled value opinion reflects the appraiser’s judgment of market value as of the effective date, giving most consideration to the approaches best supported by data and by typical market participant analysis.
